name: Borges Square Pixel Regular
classification_notes: A single-weight pixel typeface built to evoke labyrinthine structure rather than legibility; its maze-like stroke construction and square pixel grid place it firmly in the display/experimental category.
summary: A single-style pixel typeface by Matteo Bertin that treats each letter as a fragment of a labyrinth, deliberately sacrificing legibility for disorienting visual texture.
description: Designed by Matteo Bertin and released in 2026 under the SIL Open Font License, Borges takes its cue from the Borgesian labyrinth as a metaphor for writing. Each character is constructed on a square pixel grid so that a block of text reads less as language and more as a dense, maze-like pattern. The result sits somewhere between typeface and textile, useful for posters or identity work where visual density and conceptual weight matter more than reading comfort.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Borges as born from the idea of turning writing into a pattern, framing it as an attempt to capture the image of the labyrinth in a typeface. Readability is deliberately set aside in favour of a disorienting effect, and this first release explores the balance between black and white, strokes and spaces, seeking a dense and harmonious texture that stays alive and unpredictable.

name: Ronzino
classification_notes: Positioned as a reworking of Arial, Ronzino belongs firmly in the neo-grotesque tradition: anonymous, information-driven letterforms with even stroke weight and minimal personality markers.
summary: A free neo-grotesque by Luigi Gorlero and Nunzio Mazzaferro that reworks Arial's proportions while keeping its anonymous, information-driven character.
description: Built on the skeleton of Arial but with attention paid to its longstanding shortcomings, Ronzino aims for the same neutral, workaday register without the compromises of its source material. The family spans three weights with obliques, includes small capitals and alternate letterforms for 'a' and 'g', and covers extended Latin. Released under the SIL Open Font License, it positions itself as a free, considered alternative to the ubiquitous grotesques that dominate default system stacks.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Ronzino as rising from the ashes of their former brand typeface, Arial, bringing a new spin on the cult classic. It improves on Arial's many drawbacks while retaining a similar information-driven feeling: anonymous and nondescript. Designed by Luigi Gorlero and Nunzio Mazzaferro and released in January 2025, it comprises one family in six styles with Latin support and is licensed under the SIL Open Font License.

name: Aujournuit
classification_notes: A variable display typeface with calligraphic roots and organic, ink-like forms. Its expressive, atmospheric character and extreme width range place it firmly in the display category rather than text or script.
summary: A variable display typeface by Teo Gaudet drawing on calligraphic letterforms and the look of runny ink, spanning from very condensed to very wide.
description: Inspired by a 1956 French magazine title, Aujournuit stretches from an extremely condensed to a very wide setting via a variable width axis, with five named styles including an Airy variant. Its letterforms carry the looseness of calligraphy and wet ink, and the family includes all zodiac signs as supplementary glyphs. Released under the SIL Open Font Licence, it is free to download from Collletttivo.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Aujournuit as a display font built on calligraphic traits, organic shapes and runny ink, inspired by the 1956 magazine title Chemins Perdus. The name plays on the French words for today and night, referencing biodynamic farming practices tied to natural cycles. Designed by Teo Gaudet and released in December 2024, it is a variable font supporting one family across five styles with Latin coverage, licensed under the SIL Open Font Licence.

name: Absans Regular
classification_notes: A single-weight grotesque sans with deliberate nods to early 20th-century grotesque conventions, including subtle irregularities that recall pre-war European type. Collletttivo describe it as born from the need for a universal communication tool.
summary: A single-weight grotesque by Valerio Monopoli that draws quietly on early 20th-century European type conventions, released under the SIL Open Font License.
description: Designed by Valerio Monopoli and released in December 2023, the typeface carries the low-key irregularities characteristic of pre-war grotesques without leaning into pastiche. Available in one weight only, it suits restrained editorial and typographic compositions where a hint of historical character is wanted without overt decoration. Freely downloadable under the SIL Open Font License with broad Latin Extended coverage.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Absans as born from the need for a universal and unassuming communication tool, yet note that it clearly nods to early 20th-century grotesque fonts and their quaint yet captivating flaws. They position it as suited to delicate and elegant compositions, with subtle, distinctive charm that appeals to casual readers and type enthusiasts alike.

name: Mazius Display
classification_notes: A high-contrast serif with old-style proportions and a marked calligraphic quality drawn from chancery hand traditions; its extreme contrast and display orientation place it firmly in the display serif category.
summary: A high-contrast display serif with old-style proportions and a chancery calligraphic character, released free under the SIL Open Font License.
description: Designed by Alberto Casagrande and released in 2018, Mazius Display draws on chancery hand traditions to produce a serif with strong stroke contrast and old-style proportions. The family spans four styles, pairing an upright Roman and Bold with two distinct italic cuts, each carrying its own personality. Best used at large sizes for headlines, with the three styles combined for expressive typographic contrast.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Mazius Display as a high-contrast serif with old-style proportions and a strong calligraphic feel, influenced by chancery hands and featuring two italics each with a distinct personality. They recommend using it at large sizes for sleek headlines and combining the three styles together for maximum expressiveness.

name: Ribes
classification_notes: Ribes is explicitly described as a reverse-stress typeface with decorative intent and display sizing, placing it firmly in the display category with a reverse-contrast subcategory drawing on 1950s and Funk aesthetics.
summary: A reverse-stress display typeface with mixed proportions and an irreverent personality drawing on 1950s and Funk visual culture.
description: Designed by Luigi Gorlero and released in 2018, Ribes comes in three weights — Light, Regular and Black — each calibrated to deliver a different degree of warmth and directness. The reverse-stress construction, mixed proportions and deliberately confused historical references give it a character suited to headlines and large-scale typesetting. It is free to use under the SIL Open Font License.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Ribes Black as a reverse-stress typeface with mixed proportions and confused historical references, borrowing from Fifties and Funk aesthetics. The family develops its personality across three weights with varying degrees of warmth and in-your-faceness, and is intended for display sizes, though it is noted to not shy away from uncomfortable situations.

name: Mattone
classification_notes: Mattone is a sans-serif with pronounced geometric qualities, characterised by maximally round curves and generous widths. Its display origins and loud proportions align it with geometric sans conventions rather than humanist or grotesque traditions.
summary: A wide, round geometric sans with three weights, redrawn in 2021 to extend its range from display to smaller text sizes.
description: Originally conceived as a display face in 2017, Mattone was rebuilt from scratch in 2021, gaining broader language support and improved text performance at smaller sizes. Its defining character comes from generous width proportions and curves pushed as far toward circular as possible. Three weights — Regular, Bold and Black — are available under the SIL Open Font Licence.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Mattone as a highly visible sans serif with generous width proportions and loud curves, as round as they can be. Initially started in 2017 as a display face, it was completely redrawn in 2021 and can now set longer text pieces at smaller sizes with broader language support.

name: Coconat
classification_notes: Coconat features flared terminals rather than full bracketed serifs, sitting between a humanist sans and a serif; the extreme oblique stress in the Bold weight pushes it toward display territory.
summary: A three-weight Latin family by Sara Lavazza that shifts from a flared text style in Regular to a fat, oblique-stressed display face in Bold.
description: Designed by Sara Lavazza and released in 2019 under the SIL Open Font License, Coconat spans three styles whose letterforms stay consistent while the stress angle becomes increasingly extreme. The Regular and Demi are fair-proportioned with flared terminals, making them serviceable at text sizes, whereas the Bold is openly display-only, its wide set and pronounced oblique stress giving it a bold nautical character. The family covers extended Latin including accented characters for a wide range of European languages.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Coconat as a non-conventional type family in which a flared, fair-proportioned regular style transforms entirely in the extended bold version. The letter drawing remains consistent across styles, but the oblique stress becomes extreme in the fat, proud display-only Bold.

name: Sinistre
classification_notes: Sinistre combines Uncial minuscule letterforms with Roman proportions and Fraktur inflections, drawing on Insular script, Celtic calligraphy and Gaelic stone carving traditions. Its historical references and sharp terminations place it firmly in the display category under the uncial subcategory.
summary: A free uncial display typeface drawing on the Book of Kells, Gaelic stone carving and Celtic calligraphy, available in three weights with Cyrillic support.
description: Designed by Jules Durand and released in 2020 under the SIL Open Font License, Sinistre blends Uncial minuscule with Roman proportions and faint Fraktur references. Soft letterforms are offset by sharp terminations, pulling the design away from straight revival and towards something usable for contemporary titling. Cyrillic support was added by Varya Goncharova, extending the family's reach beyond Latin.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Sinistre as combining Uncial minuscule, Roman proportions and subtle Fraktur hints into soft letterforms with sharp terminations. They frame it as a revival of the Insular Script of the Book of Kells, drawing on Gaelic stone carvings, Celtic calligraphy and Elzevir types, suited to contemporary titling that sits between historical reference and loftiness.

name: Apfel Grotezk
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as inspired by neo-grotesque and geometric typefaces, with easy curves and an exaggerated x-height, placing it at the intersection of those two traditions.
summary: A round, airy grotesque with an exaggerated x-height, notable for its lower-ink-consumption variant Brukt developed with SPRINT.
description: Designed by Luigi Gorlero and released in 2019, this five-style family draws on neo-grotesque and geometric traditions while leaning into a friendlier register through soft curves and a generous x-height. The Brukt sub-family introduces cut-outs of varying shapes and sizes to reduce ink coverage in print, developed in collaboration with SPRINT. Available under the SIL Open Font License, it covers Latin with broad accented character support.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Apfel Grotezk as a round and airy sans serif family inspired by neo-grotesque and geometric typefaces, with easy curves and an exaggerated x-height lending it a friendly attitude. Its companion, Apfel Grotezk Brukt, was developed in collaboration with SPRINT to reduce ink usage in printing through cut-outs of varying shapes and sizes.

name: Ortica
classification_notes: Collletttivo describe it as a calligraphic serif; the Bold subfamily is constructed entirely from straight segments inspired by Vojtech Preissig's Preissig Antiqua, while the Light subfamily uses curves with sharp, spiky serifs.
summary: A calligraphic serif in four styles split across two contrasting subfamilies, one built entirely from straight segments after Vojtech Preissig.
description: Designed by Ben Bovani and released in 2019, Ortica splits into two subfamilies: the Angular cuts, constructed solely from straight segments in the manner of Preissig Antiqua, and the Linear cuts, which use curves but retain sharp, spiky serifs. At large sizes the geometric construction is fully visible; at text sizes the family retains enough refinement for functional use. Available under the SIL Open Font Licence.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Ortica as a calligraphic serif with two contrasting but complementary personalities. The Bold draws from Czech designer Vojtech Preissig's Preissig Antiqua and is composed entirely of straight segments, while the Light appears smoother but features sharp, spiky serifs. The typeface reveals its construction and character at display sizes while remaining elegant and functional at smaller sizes.

name: Messapia
classification_notes: The family pairs a Regular drawn from a 19th-century serif model with a Bold that is described as a heavy humanist extended face, making it an experimental hybrid that resists a single conventional classification.
summary: A two-weight experimental family that deliberately pairs a 19th-century-inspired Regular with a heavy humanist extended Bold, treating contrast between styles as a design principle.
description: Designed by Luca Marsano and released in 2019, Messapia holds two styles that would ordinarily belong to entirely different typefaces. The Regular draws on 19th-century precedent, while the Bold takes the form of a heavy, wide humanist serif. Available under the SIL Open Font License, covering Latin with extended diacritic support.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Messapia as an experimental type family made up of two seemingly unrelated styles: a Regular inspired by a 19th-century typeface and a Bold that is a heavy humanist extended face.

name: Halibut
classification_notes: A roman serif with oblique stress, unbracketed serifs and wide-to-narrow width axis; the sharp, spiky serifs and variable width range place it firmly in the display serif territory with a geometric edge.
summary: A variable serif by Matteo Maggi with a wide-to-narrow width axis, oblique stress, unbracketed serifs and pronounced spiky terminals.
description: Released in 2018 and updated through 2022, Halibut spans condensed to expanded widths on a single variable axis alongside a weight axis running from Thin to Regular. The design features large counter shapes, sharp unbracketed serifs and rounded curves, making it better suited to display and titling than continuous text. Licensed under the SIL Open Font License and available as a free download from Collletttivo.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Halibut as a roman variable typeface with oblique stress, big countershapes and sharp unbracketed serifs. The family includes both very wide and very narrow versions with long spiky serifs and rounded curves, designed by Matteo Maggi and released in November 2018.

name: Sprat
classification_notes: A variable serif with long sharp serifs and high contrast between thick and thin strokes, inspired by Eric Gill's lettering. Its extreme contrast and aggressive thin styles place it firmly in display serif territory.
summary: A variable display serif by Ethan Nakache, drawn on width and weight axes with long sharp serifs and high contrast, loosely inspired by Eric Gill's lettering.
description: Running across 18 styles via a two-axis variable font, Sprat shifts character dramatically between extremes: the thin cuts read as hard and aggressive, whilst the blacks soften slightly without losing their tension. Long sharp serifs and pronounced stroke contrast make it well suited to titling, posters and logos, though mid-range weights hold up in larger body text. Released free under the SIL Open Font License.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Sprat as a sharp serif variable font developed on two axes of width and weight, inspired by an old lettering from Eric Gill. They note its long sharp serifs, high contrast and round curves, with an appearance that changes considerably between styles. Primarily intended for titling, posters and logos, though certain weights are suitable for mid-sized body text.

name: Sneaky Times
classification_notes: A reworked version of Times New Roman, itself a transitional serif; the modifications are cosmetic and conceptual rather than structural, preserving the underlying transitional serif classification.
summary: A single-weight reworking of Times New Roman by Jules Durand, embedding a hidden hand gesture as a recurring Easter egg throughout the glyph set.
description: Designed by Jules Durand and released in 2019 under the SIL Open Font License, it takes Times New Roman as its base and inserts a tiny hand performing the "Circle Game" gesture as a concealed motif. The joke originates from a schoolyard prank popularised by the US sitcom Malcolm in the Middle before spreading as an internet meme. With one weight and Latin support only, it is a novelty release rather than a workhorse typeface.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Sneaky Times as an inside joke font, a reworked Times New Roman containing a recurrent Easter egg: a small hand making a circle, referencing the Circle Game, a trickery prank invented by a child in the 1980s as a way to punch friends, later popularised by Malcolm in the Middle and spread as an internet meme.

name: Necto Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced sans-serif with low contrast, medium apertures and slanted terminals, designed explicitly as a fixed-width typeface for coding, data and technical contexts.
summary: A single-weight monospaced sans from Marco Condello, released free under the SIL Open Font License, built for technical and coding contexts.
description: Necto Mono is a low-contrast monospaced sans with medium apertures and slanted terminals, released by Italian collective Collletttivo in 2019. It covers a broad Latin character set including accented glyphs, fractions, mathematical symbols and arrows, making it practical for both programming environments and data display. Available in one weight and style, it is distributed freely under the SIL Open Font License.
foundry_blurb: Collletttivo describe Necto Mono as a sans-serif font characterised by low contrast, medium apertures and slanted terminals, designed for any kind of visual mutiny. Designed by Marco Condello and released on 28 May 2019, it is offered as a single family with one style, supporting Latin, under the SIL Open Font License.
